description: Machine-readable coverage matrix for the windowed-scenario gate. Source of truth for what is verified — append-only AUDIT_REPORT.md is a generated narrative view. See SESSION_PLAYBOOK.md for the rules of the road.
$rules: A 'covered' tile must reference at least one scenario file under tests/scenarios/.
Each tile's scenarios[] entries must exist as files; check_coverage.py enforces this.
last_verified_commit should be the most recent commit at which the listed scenarios passed.
Scenarios may cover multiple tiles via their 'covers' array; every cover tag should map to a tile here.

tiles.description: ReplayManager's recording stays bounded during a long AI-vs-AI game: phase-transition snapshots use the history-free gameplay snapshot and are capped (oldest non-initial snapshots dropped); recorded action events carry no _replay_diffs/_ai_* payloads (diffs are stored once, separately).
GameState.state["history"] stays capped during a long AI-vs-AI game, and phase_log/history actions are stored without their _replay_diffs/_ai_* payloads.
AIPlayer's whole-game accumulators (_all_decision_records, _action_log) stay within their bounded caps during a long AI-vs-AI game.
GameEventLog.entries stays within its bounded cap during a long AI-vs-AI game.
ActionLogger.session_actions stays within its bounded cap, and retained actions carry no _replay_diffs/_ai_decision_records payloads, during a long AI-vs-AI game.

During a Charge move the attached CHARACTER of a charging unit (e.g. a Blade Champion leading Custodian Guard) is draggable like its squadmates. ChargeController keys all movement bookkeeping by a charge key (bare model id for the bodyguard, '<char_unit>:<model>' for character models) and hit-tests in board space against each model's real base radius, nearest-wins.

APPLY_CHARGE_MOVE (and the Heroic Intervention variant) accepts composite '<char_unit>:<model>' per_model_paths keys for manually dragged attached-character models, resolved via _resolve_charge_path_ref (only units actually attached to the charger are accepted). All charge validators and both apply loops handle them; auto-ride skips manually placed characters.

Player can drag the attached leader into position during a charge instead of relying on the automatic post-confirm ride-along; the leader lands at the dragged spot with charged_this_turn/fights_first, and an unmoved leader still follows the squad automatically.

Duplicate squads sharing a meta.name (e.g. two Boyz squads) get DISTINCT auto-assigned token colors. GameState.auto_assign_unit_color picks the first faction-palette color unused army-wide, falling back to same-name-unused when the palette is exhausted. Regression guard for the hex-roundtrip compare bug where Color.is_equal_approx never matched the stored 8-bit hex, so every unit came out palette[0].

Settings > Visual 'Unit Color' = 'Ring Only' (default) makes TokenVisual draw the unit color as a ring inside a neutral base instead of filling the whole base. Toggling SettingsService.unit_color_display_mode emits unit_color_display_changed and reaches the live board token, asserted via TokenVisual._is_ring_color_mode().

In-game 'AI Speed' OptionButton in the top menu bar (HUD_Bottom/HBoxContainer, rendered as the top row) next to the AI Suggestion button. Reveals itself through the natural Main._ready() flow for single-viewer human-vs-AI games; a real click opens the popup and keyboard navigation selects a preset that drives AIPlayer.set_ai_speed_preset.

AI speed preset switching stays in sync both ways: picking a preset from the top-menu dropdown updates AIPlayer's live preset, and changes from the < > adjust / cycle hotkeys and programmatic set_ai_speed_preset (the save/load path) update the dropdown selection via the ai_speed_changed signal.

HUD affordance: the AI speed dropdown sits in the top menu row next to the AI Suggestion button, follows the same visibility rule (single-viewer human-vs-AI only), exposes the four presets (Fast/Normal/Slow/Step-by-step), and its selected item matches the live AIPlayer preset.

Board token mouseover tooltip: hovering any model (own or opponent, anywhere on the board) shows the unit's real name, stats (M/T/Sv/W/OC from meta.stats), model count and wound totals; hovering empty board hides it. Regression guard for the screen-vs-board coordinate mixup that limited hover to one board region and the meta.* schema keys that had shown 'Unknown' / '?'.
EffectPrimitives PLUS_CHARGE effect — sets effect_plus_charge flag on a unit
EffectPrimitives: Feel No Pain (effect_fnp=N) flag triggers an FNP roll for each wound before damage applies. Validated by Supa-Cybork Body enhancement (#374).
EffectPrimitives: effect_fall_back_and_shoot=true overrides the rule that a unit which Fell Back cannot shoot/charge that turn. Validated by Kunnin' But Brutal enhancement (#374).

LeaderPairingsLoader: data-driven lookup of which CHARACTER units can be attached to which bodyguard units. Loaded from data/leader_pairings.csv and queried by FormationsPhase during DECLARE_LEADER_ATTACHMENT (#378).

Measurement.model_outside_board(pos, model): single source of truth for the off-board rule. Builds a base shape at pos, translates the local bounds by pos, and checks the resulting world-space bounding rect against the battlefield (SettingsService.get_board_width_px/get_board_height_px). Returns false safely if SettingsService is missing.
RulesEngine: weapons with effect_devastating_wounds=true convert critical wounds (unmodified 6 to wound) into mortal wounds that bypass saves. Validated by Headwoppa's Killchoppa (#374).
RulesEngine: weapons with effect_ignores_cover=true neutralize the +1 cover save modifier. Validated by Panoptispex enhancement (#374).
RulesEngine: returns the per-shot damage bonus from the WAAAGH!-energy 'Eadbanger ability. Validated by '387_waaagh_energy_eadbanger.
RulesEngine: when a unit with the Deadly Demise ability is destroyed, rolls the demise dice and applies mortal-wound damage to nearby units. Validated by '386_deadly_demise_vehicle.

RulesEngine: validates a SHOOT action against the current shooter state — eligibility, target legality, weapon assignments. Validated by '370_bgnt_vehicle_shoots_in_engagement (Big Guns Never Tire seam).
TurnManager: chooses which player deploys first when the deployment phase opens. The defender always deploys first per the 10e rules. Validated by '377_defender_deploys_first.

Regression guard (#426): INFANTRY charging through a ruin must not pay the climb penalty. Callers of TerrainManager.calculate_charge_terrain_penalty used to omit unit_keywords, so the helper defaulted to applying the climb cost even to INFANTRY (which can traverse ruins freely). Validated by 'charge_infantry_through_ruins.

CHARGE phase: TerrainManager.calculate_charge_terrain_penalty returns 0" extra distance when the charging unit has the INFANTRY keyword and the intervening terrain is traversable (e.g. ruins), regardless of height_category. Validated by 'charge_infantry_through_ruins.

CHARGE phase: TerrainManager.calculate_charge_terrain_penalty applies NO difficult_ground penalty to units that can move through the piece (e.g. INFANTRY through ruins), matching calculate_movement_terrain_penalty. Non-traversing units (VEHICLE/MONSTER) still pay the flat 2" per piece; callers omitting unit_keywords keep the legacy everyone-pays behaviour. Validated by 'charge_infantry_difficult_ground'.

Regression guard (user report 2026-07-16): INFANTRY charging while inside/through the difficult_ground area ruins of the converted 11e layouts was charged a flat 2" per overlapping piece on top of the drag distance ('Effective: 16.1" (10.1" - 6.0" terrain)'), inflating Used/Left and rejecting valid drops. The windowed scenario drives the real drag_board + Confirm Charge Moves path and fails on pre-fix code. Validated by 'charge_infantry_difficult_ground'.

Regression guard: ChargePhase._validate_charge_movement_constraints rejects any charge whose final per-model position overlaps a terrain wall (FAIL_WALL via _validate_no_wall_overlaps). Charging models may pass through walls during the move but may not end on one. Closes the server-side client/server divergence (drag UI gated it, but server-authoritative APPLY_CHARGE_MOVE / heroic-intervention did not). Validated by 'charge_cannot_end_on_wall.
Charge-roll modifier flag (effect_plus_charge) honoured by DECLARE_CHARGE — 'ERE WE GO stratagem path

CHARGE phase: when the charging unit's destination is congested by friendly/enemy tokens, models still attempt to reach engagement range via the closest legal positioning. Validated by 'charge_congestion.
HEROIC INTERVENTION post-charge-into-engagement trigger, USE_HEROIC_INTERVENTION 2D6 charge, CP -1
CommandPhase: at the start of each player's command phase, both players gain CP per the 10e rules (1 CP each, capped at 15). Validated by '382_cp_grant_both_players.
DeploymentPhase: a unit marked by the Castellan's mark stratagem may be redeployed once before the first turn begins. Validated by '397_castellan_mark_redeploy.
DeploymentPhase: per the 10e rules, the player chosen as defender deploys first. Validated by '377_defender_deploys_first.

Issue #87: no part of any model may extend off the battlefield. Enforced via Measurement.model_outside_board, called from DeploymentController/MovementController/ChargeController/FightController and from DeploymentPhase._validate_model_position as defence-in-depth against dispatched DEPLOY_UNIT actions.

Inverse of deployment.no_offboard_placement: a model whose base lies wholly inside the active player's deployment zone must be placed without tripping the off-board check. Regression guard for the Blade Champion bug where Measurement.model_outside_board treated shape.get_bounds() (local-space, centered on origin) as world coords and so rejected every placement.

DeploymentController.try_place_at(world_pos): the controller entry point invoked by the player's click on the board during the Deployment phase. Verifies that a valid in-zone click populates temp_positions / spawns the preview token instead of being silently rejected by an upstream guard (e.g. Measurement.model_outside_board).

End-to-end input path for the Deployment phase Confirm button (HUD_Right/.../ConfirmButton): after a model is placed, a real simulated mouse click on the button must reach _on_confirm_pressed and commit the unit (status -> DEPLOYED). Regression guard for later-z overlay panels (UnitStatsPanel) eating the click via default MOUSE_FILTER_STOP.

LeftRoster (T37) duplicates the unit list already shown in HUD_Right and used to overlap GameLogPanel on the left edge. Hidden by default; a 'Roster' button in HUD_Bottom/HBoxContainer plus the L hotkey reveal it. When shown it raises itself via move_to_front so its cards aren't obscured by the GameLogPanel.

SecondaryMissionPanel used to anchor at top-left (8, 8) and float over LeftRoster / GameLogPanel as a 300x32 collapsed bar. It now anchors at top-center under the HUD top bar, is hidden by default, and a 'Missions' button in HUD_Bottom/HBoxContainer plus the M hotkey reveal it. Reveal also auto-expands the panel so the player sees mission content immediately.
GameLogPanel's bottom edge sits flush with the viewport bottom. Previously offset_bottom = -45 left a 45px gap which looked broken; now offset_bottom = 0.

UnitStatsPanel is anchored BOTTOM_WIDE and overlaps the bottom strip of HUD_Right where the deployment buttons live. The panel and its layout-only descendants (VBox, Header, Spacer) must be MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE so the deployment buttons remain reachable; the ToggleButton keeps its own STOP so the panel still toggles.

The Save & Load dialog is a modal menu and must render above every HUD panel (GameLogPanel, LeftRosterStrip). Main sets up the dialog early in _ready() and _ensure_ui_panels_on_top() bumps all HUD Controls to UI_PANEL_Z, so without an explicit modal z_index the later-added GameLogPanel won the tree-order tie-break and drew over it. Fixed by giving the dialog z_index = UI_MODAL_Z at setup and move_to_front() in show_dialog() (front-most sibling => draws on top and receives input in the overlap region).

Pre-deployment roll-off (issue #85): both players roll a D6. The winner chooses to be Attacker or Defender — Defender deploys first and takes the second turn, Attacker deploys second and takes the first turn.

Regression guard: Measurement.model_overlaps_any_wall rejects every model (INFANTRY/VEHICLE/MONSTER/FLY alike) whose base overlaps a wall segment at deployment time. Path-traversal continues to honor unit keywords (infantry may pass through walls during movement); this guard covers only the endpoint rule. Reverses prior #431 interpretation that let INFANTRY end deployment overlapping walls. Validated by 'infantry_enters_ruin_wall.
FIGHT phase: the Deadly Demise ability fires when a unit is destroyed during fight, rolling demise dice and dealing mortal wounds to nearby units. Validated by '386_deadly_demise_vehicle.
FIGHT phase: the Headwoppa's Killchoppa enhancement grants devastating-wounds and charged_this_turn to the bearer. Validated by '374_headwoppa_devastating_wounds.

11e Counter-Offensive updates the fight sequence (active_fighter_id, current_selecting_player). CO stratagem mechanics are covered headless by test_core_stratagems_11e.gd; a windowed 11e CO scenario (fight resolves -> CO offered -> USE) is a follow-up after the 10e co_offer_after_charge scenario was retired in the 10e->11e fight cleanup.
Fight-phase AI does not select own units as melee targets — observational scenario captures before/after screenshots of AI fight decisions

COUNTER-OFFENSIVE stratagem: USE_COUNTER_OFFENSIVE effect, CP -2. Mechanics covered headless by test_core_stratagems_11e.gd; a windowed 11e CO scenario is a follow-up after the 10e co_offer_after_charge scenario was retired in the 10e->11e fight cleanup.
ScenarioRunner autoload boot, fixture load, scene swap, RNG seeding, state walking, screenshot capture

Issue #51: the Movement Phase right panel section 3 now exposes a Confirm Movement Mode button that dispatches LOCK_MOVEMENT_MODE for the active unit. Once locked, mode-radio CheckBoxes disable and the unit cannot switch mode until movement completes.
MOVEMENT phase: Da Jump teleports a unit to within 9" of the warlord and ≥9" from enemies. Bounds check rejects invalid placements. Validated by '376_da_jump_bounds.
MOVEMENT phase: Follow Me Ladz enhancement grants +2 to the bearer's Move characteristic (effect_plus_move). Validated by '394_follow_me_ladz_plus_move.
MOVEMENT phase: Kunnin' But Brutal enhancement grants effect_fall_back_and_shoot and effect_fall_back_and_charge to the bearer. Validated by '374_kunnin_but_brutal_fallback.
MOVEMENT phase: a 20-model horde unit successfully moves toward a target despite congestion from blocking units. Validated by 'horde_movement.
RAPID INGRESS end-of-movement trigger, reserves filter, USE_RAPID_INGRESS, CP -1

Regression guard: MovementPhase rejects ANY model drop whose base overlaps a wall segment at the final position — no unit may end its movement on top of a wall. Infantry may still pass through walls mid-move (path-traversal honors unit keywords); this guard covers only the endpoint rule. Reverses prior #431 interpretation that let INFANTRY end movement overlapping walls (observed broken in-game: Witchseekers sitting on the north wall of Grey Ruins). Validated by 'infantry_enters_ruin_wall.
Multi-peer deployment sync — host deploys, client mirrors positions and coherency state
Multi-peer dice broadcast — defender re-emits result.dice on remote (T5-MP5)
Multi-peer network session lifecycle — host/join/disconnect, peer registration
Multi-peer save broadcast — broadcast_id + retry budget + dedupe (T5-MP4-RELIABILITY)
Multi-peer shooting visual broadcast — SELECT_SHOOTER/ASSIGN_TARGET/CONFIRM/COMPLETE (T5-MP3)
CommandPhase._generate_command_points: grants the per-turn CP to both players according to the 10e rules. Validated by '382_cp_grant_both_players.
DeploymentPhase.CASTELLAN_REDEPLOY action: applies the Castellan's mark redeploy effect, moving the marked unit to a new valid deployment position. Validated by '397_castellan_mark_redeploy.
FormationsPhase.DESIGNATE_WARLORD action: marks one CHARACTER as the player's warlord (is_warlord=true) and clears the flag on any prior warlord. Validated by '367_designate_warlord.
FormationsPhase.DECLARE_LEADER_ATTACHMENT action: pairs a CHARACTER leader with a bodyguard unit for the duration of the battle. Validated by '378_leader_pairing_formations.
10e: Lone Operative is a targeting restriction (not an attachment ban). LO+Leader characters CAN attach to bodyguards. FormationsPhase _validate_declare_leader_attachment honours this

MovementPhase.LOCK_MOVEMENT_MODE action: marks active_moves[unit].mode_locked=true so subsequent mode-change attempts are rejected for that unit this turn. Emits movement_mode_locked signal which MovementController listens for to disable mode radios.
MovementPhase.PLACE_DA_JUMP action: handles the Da Jump teleport, checking 9" warlord and 9" enemy bounds. Validated by '376_da_jump_bounds.
MovementPhase: applies effect_plus_move to the unit's Move characteristic during the movement step. Validated by '394_follow_me_ladz_plus_move.

RollOffPhase.CHOOSE_DEPLOYMENT action: the pre-deployment roll-off winner chooses to deploy first (Defender) or second (Attacker); sets meta.attacker / meta.defender so TurnManager._handle_deployment_phase_start gives the defender first deploy. Does NOT set meta.first_turn_player (that is the separate first-turn roll-off).

RollOffPhase.ROLL_OFF_DEPLOYMENT action: roll a D6 for each player to decide who deploys first, declare a winner, or report a tie that triggers a re-roll. Supports dice_roll override for deterministic testing.

FirstTurnRollOffPhase.ROLL_OFF_FIRST_TURN action (10e roll-off #2, post-deployment): both players roll a D6; the higher roll TAKES THE FIRST TURN (no choice) and is stored in meta.first_turn_player; ties re-roll.

FirstTurnRollOffPhase.CONFIRM_FIRST_TURN action: acknowledgement that completes the first-turn roll-off phase (the human clicks Continue; the AI auto-confirms in AI-vs-AI). Keeps the dramatic result on screen until dismissed.

Regression guard (#425): ScoutPhase._validate_set_scout_model_dest rejects a destination that overlaps a sibling model — either a sibling's already-staged scout-move position or an unmoved sibling's original position. A clearly non-overlapping destination still succeeds. Validated by 'scout_no_overlap.

ShootingPhase._can_unit_shoot: returns true unless the unit has a hard blocker (Fell Back without override, etc.). Battle-shock no longer blocks shooting in 10e. Validated by '383_battleshock_can_shoot.

Pre-game warlord designation: each player picks one CHARACTER as their warlord during the formations phase. Required by FormationsPhase._validate_warlord_designation before CONFIRM_FORMATIONS will succeed. Validated by '367_designate_warlord.
T2-1 — STEALTH ability: -1 to hit on ranged attacks vs Stealth target
Issue #336 — no CP on R1 first command phase; otherwise active player only
T1-1 — MELTA X auto-resolves damage bonus at half range
T1-2 — TWIN-LINKED re-rolls all failed wound rolls
T2-M6 — base-touching regression (#321/#327)
Issue #329 — RNGService.test_mode_seed produces identical dice across runs; new helpers (randi/randi_range/randf) honor it; TransportManager + MissionManager + Mathhammer plumbed
Issue #338 — FactionAbilityManager + StratagemManager state round-trips through save/load
T2-S7 — Cover save bonus capped: Sv3+ vs AP0 in cover stays 3+ (was 2+)
Issue #356 — Multipotentiality stratagem: effect_fall_back_and_shoot overrides cannot_shoot gate in ShootingPhase + RulesEngine.validate_shoot
T2-S4..S6 — SUSTAINED HITS / LETHAL HITS / DEVASTATING WOUNDS keyword pipelines

Issue #359 — FactionStratagemLoader._map_target correctly handles 'excluding X' as not_keyword:X (was incorrectly mapping to keyword:X). Affects 'ARD AS NAILS, UNWAVERING SENTINELS, ARCHEOTECH MUNITIONS
SHOOTING phase: the WAAAGH!-energy 'Eadbanger ability grants a per-shot damage bonus during a Waaagh!-active turn. Validated by '387_waaagh_energy_eadbanger.
SHOOTING phase: in 10e, battle-shock units can still shoot (the 9e carryover that blocked shooting was removed). Validated by '383_battleshock_can_shoot.
SHOOTING phase: Big Guns Never Tire allows monster/vehicle units to shoot ranged weapons while in engagement range (at -1 to hit). Validated by '370_bgnt_vehicle_shoots_in_engagement.
SHOOTING phase: the Panoptispex enhancement gives the bearer's ranged attacks effect_ignores_cover. Validated by '374_panoptispex_ignores_cover.
SHOOTING phase: the Supa-Cybork Body enhancement grants effect_fnp=4 (Feel No Pain 4+) to the bearer. Validated by '374_supa_cybork_fnp.

_sync_all_token_positions correctly handles nested (wrapper->TokenVisual) deployment tokens.
AI does not auto-resolve the mutual roll-off while a human is present.
AI-vs-AI at ed.11 progresses through phases/turns under 11e template gating (forward-progress asserted).
RulesEngine.get_eligible_shooter_models returns the bearers eligible to split onto a target.
SettingsService.set_auto_allocate_wounds(true) is honoured by the overlay.
TransportManager tracks embarked unit and disembark eligibility.
Post-deployment 'Determine First Turn' roll-off driven via real Roll+Continue clicks; winner takes first turn.
Fully place a unit in TIGHT then switch to SINGLE: set_formation_mode no longer creates a data-less invisible ghost; subsequent click still works.
Place 1 in SINGLE, switch TIGHT (place 2), switch back SINGLE: no invisible ghost spawned and clicks still place (get_placed_count progresses).
Deploying a multi-model-type unit (Lootas) renders the Select-Model-Type picker inside the right-hand unit card (has_model_type_picker true + screenshot).
CONFIRM_UNIT_MOVE does not re-apply hazards at ed.11 (alive count unchanged after begin).
is_at_half_strength_combined drives the ed.11 trigger (sole trigger at exactly 5/10).
Deploying a reserve scout into its DZ sets the unit DEPLOYED (status 2).
MainMenu boots with terrain defaulting to the default matchup's first official 11e variant (take_and_hold_mirror_1) and deployment following that layout's paired pattern (live OptionButton widgets).
The per-model reach circle grows to the advanced cap (base6+advance4=10in) in place when the advance roll raises the cap.
Reach overlay is per-picked-up-model (8 dash segments + 1 label), anchored at the model, not a unit-wide bubble.
INFANTRY may stage a move into a tall ruin; BEGIN_NORMAL_MOVE+STAGE_MODEL_MOVE accepted (no phantom climb cost).
_get_vertical_climb_cost returns 0 for INFANTRY (regression: full terrain height no longer added).

ChargeMove11e.eligible honours advance-and-charge / fall-back-and-charge ability overrides (Waaagh!, Full Throttle, Adrenaline Junkies) and the turbo_boosted hard lock, matching ChargePhase._can_unit_charge so an advanced Waaagh! unit is offered a charge.

A unit that Advanced during an active Waaagh! can declare a charge: the Waaagh!'s effect_advance_and_charge (and 5+ invuln) survive the Shooting phase's end-of-phase effect-flag clear and last until the owner's next Command phase.
DisembarkMove template enforces tactical/combat set-up distances.
FallBackMove ordered_retreat vs desperate_escape modes (desperate rolls hazards).
IngressMove template enforces 6in-from-edge + 9in-from-enemy and stamps reinforcement flags.
MoveTypes.available_for gates which move actions a unit is offered.
Ed.11 charge flow: declare with empty targets is legal (11.02), roll computes selectable targets, apply rejects out-of-range target.
CommandPhase queues battle-shock tests; at-exactly-half-strength triggers at ed.11 (08.03) but not ed.10.
Ed.11 FightPhase defers selection to FightSequencer (12.04): alternation starts with active player; out-of-turn select refused.
Ed.11 disembark modes (18.04): stationary transport forces TACTICAL 3in (4in rejected); COMBAT fallback accepts 6in + hazard + battle-shock.
Ed.11 ingress (20.04): mid-board placement rejected; edge placement >8in from enemies succeeds with arrived_from_reserves + no_moves_until_charge.
At ed.11 MovementPhase offers MoveType templates; offering flips live when engagement changes (advance<->fall back).
BEGIN_ADVANCE dispatched live; pauses awaiting command-reroll decision.
DECLINE_COMMAND_REROLL resolves the advance roll and emits unit_move_begun -> MovementController move cap updates.
_update_movement_display_with_advance redraws the SAME MoveRangeVisual overlay to the new radius (asserted radius + label + screenshot).
_show_model_range_overlay / _clear_move_range_overlay draw and clear the MoveRangeVisual on pickup/drop.
Battle-shocked fall-back applies 06.03 desperate-escape hazards once at BEGIN_FALL_BACK.
Completing Formations (CONFIRM_FORMATIONS both players) transitions the live phase to ROLL_OFF (3).
CONFIRM_SCOUT_MOVE action commits staged positions into GameState.units[].models[].position and survives into the movement phase snapshot.
END_SCOUT_PHASE action (AI/MCP path) commits staged scout moves into GameState (token at moved pos after transition).
END_SCOUT_PHASE rejects an incoherent staged config and leaves the phase SCOUT so the player can fix it.
Clicking the REAL Confirm button (pressed signal -> _on_scout_confirm_pressed -> CONFIRM_SCOUT_MOVE) writes staged positions into GameState.
After re-selecting a scout unit clears _scout_active_unit_id, the REAL Confirm button still commits staged positions to GameState.
Invalid scout confirm shows a toast and _sync_all_token_positions rolls the NESTED deployment token's visual back to state pos.
Selecting a scout unit from the right-panel list wires scout state (confirm button shown/connected) and the REAL Confirm commits; no fall-through to movement branch.
Selecting a scout unit via the stats panel sets _scout_active_unit_id and enables drag; REAL Confirm commits.
Real scout drag handlers (motion+release) stage moves; ending the phase via the real phase-action button commits them (token + state at moved pos).

Regression guard: the battle round advances after the round's SECOND player ends their turn (GameState.is_last_turn_of_round), not unconditionally after Player 2. Reinforcements (Strategic Reserves / Deep Strike) are offered in the movement phase only from Battle Round 2 onward, for whichever player takes the first turn. Positive control confirms reserves DO appear in Round 2. Validated by 'first_turn_p2_no_reserves_turn_one'.

The movement unit list offers ZERO reinforcement rows on the first turn of the game even when the active player holds units in reserves — including when Player 2 took the first turn and Player 1 is now taking the game's first turn. Asserted via MovementController.get_reinforcement_row_count(). Validated by 'first_turn_p2_no_reserves_turn_one'.

Windowed regression: Main._recreate_unit_visuals() guards against overlapping async runs, so two refreshes landing in one frame no longer rebuild the token set twice and leave duplicate/overlapping model tokens (the pile-in 'ghost'). Verified by pile_in_no_ghost_token.
Windowed regression: moving a model during Fight-phase pile-in/consolidate no longer leaves a duplicate of it at its original position. Verified by pile_in_no_ghost_token.

COORD-4: the AI's army-wide movement plan is computed once per movement phase and CONSUMED as units act — the 'Movement plan' block announced in the game log matches the movement actions logged afterwards. Verified by ai_battle_plan_log_integrity.

The executed per-unit movement actions logged in the game log match the announced 'Movement plan' block — GameEventLog.movement_plan_integrity() zero mismatches over >=3 checked actions (COORD-4 gate).
When an AI decision card's chosen option scores lower than a rejected one, the card carries a 'why not the top score' coordination explanation instead of leaving the contradiction unexplained.
GameLogPanel renders AI thinking blocks as collapsible cards during a live AI movement phase (asserted via GameEventLog entry counts + screenshots in the battle-plan integrity scenario).

FIGHT phase (11e 12.04): get_available_actions offers SELECT_FIGHTER for exactly the FightSequencer's current candidates, tagged with the picking player, instead of the legacy fight_sequence queue that desynced once fights happened out of order. Regression for the 2026-07-12 report where an engaged Stompa was never offered its fight (the AI acted on a stale offer and END_FIGHT forfeited every remaining fight). Validated by 'stompa_gets_to_fight_11e'.

FightSequencer.peek_selection() — a pure, non-mutating preview of next_selection() used by get_available_actions on every UI/AI poll; must not advance picker/step. Validated by 'stompa_gets_to_fight_11e' (live) and test_iss050_fight_phase_11e.gd (headless purity/parity).

A long simple GameLogPanel entry (>200 chars, e.g. a verbose AI move rationale like 'Prosecutors moves toward obj_center …') renders as a truncated preview carrying a VISIBLE 'show more' toggle instead of silently trailing off. Validated by 'game_log_long_entry_expandable' (collapsed: last_card_is_expandable == true, last_card_is_expanded == false, small visible char count + screenshot).

Expanding a truncated GameLogPanel entry via its 'show more' toggle swaps in the full text (button flips to 'show less') and is reversible. Validated by 'game_log_long_entry_expandable' (expand_last_card -> last_card_is_expanded == true, visible char count jumps from ~201 to the full ~302 + screenshot).

11e per-line 13.10/13.11 block test: 'area'-class pieces obscure (light/dense) unless their area GROUP is in the exclusion set; 'feature'-class dense pieces are Solid at ground level; legacy bare pieces are both. Regression net for the Custodian-vs-Gretchin 'unit on a terrain strip claimed no LoS' report.

Per observer/target pair, the terrain-area groups either model is within (ANY part of base, 01.04) — the 13.10 'excluding areas one or both models are within' set — plus the feature ids a model OCCUPIES (base centre inside) so a structure never Solid-blocks its own occupant's sight lines.

EnhancedLineOfSight's edition-11 per-line judgement: 13.10 obscuring areas with the within-group exclusion + 13.11 dense feature Solid at ground level + explicit wall segments, replacing the 10e ruins/height rules while keeping base-to-base sampling (06.01).

Shooting eligibility: a unit with models standing ON a terrain area is targetable from outside it when within the shooter's detection range (13.09/13.10 gates applied via the enhanced LoS path). Driven end-to-end through the shooting UI.

13.09 HIDDEN qualification: INFANTRY/BEASTS/SWARM within a terrain-area GROUP that contains a dense feature (any part of base), unit hasn't shot this or last turn. Group-aware so a model on one linked boundary half counts the whole area's dense pieces.

13.09 visibility gate: a hidden model is only visible to observers within its detection range (15" default, Gone-to-Ground/datasheet modified). Gone-to-Ground no longer penalises for dense pieces of the target's own area.

Player-facing shooting-refusal reason honours the 11e gates: names the Hidden rule with detection range + closest-model distance, or 'terrain blocks every sight line', instead of a bare 'No line of sight'.

CHARGE phase target declaration selection model: the ELIGIBLE TARGETS ItemList's own selection state is the single source of truth for selected_targets (multi_selected/empty_clicked signals; the old item_selected hookup never fired in SELECT_MULTI mode and its gui_input hack accumulated every clicked row, silently declaring multi-charges). Plain click selects exactly one target, Ctrl+Click toggles targets for a deliberate multi-charge, empty-click clears, the Declare button shows the target count, and rows lock once the charge is declared. A prominent, dynamic gold-bordered hint (target_hint_label) spells out Ctrl+Click and updates with the selection (0 targets: 'Ctrl+Click each one'; 1: 'hold Ctrl+Click another target'; 2+: 'N units — Ctrl+Click to add/remove'), hidden once declared. Validated by 'charge_target_declaration'.

DECLARE_CHARGE with multiple target_unit_ids stores ALL declared targets in pending_charges, and the stored arrays are duplicates — mutating the controller's live selected_targets after declaring (sentinel append) must not alter the pending charge. Validated by 'charge_target_declaration'.

11e 19.03: a CHARACTER attached to a bodyguard is ONE Attached unit in the fight phase's global Pile In (12.02) and Consolidate (12.07) steps — one picker entry named 'Bodyguard + Character', one move covering both units' models (payload keys 'char_unit:index'), union eligibility/engagement, and rejection of a direct PILE_IN/CONSOLIDATE for the attached character. Headless: tests/test_pile_in_attached_characters_11e.gd.

FightController pile-in/consolidate interactive mode seeds, drags, snaps and submits the attached characters' models alongside the bodyguard's ('unit:model' tracking keys) so the player moves the whole Attached unit in one pile-in.

11e 19.02: a CHARACTER attached to a bodyguard is part of ONE Attached unit and can never be selected as a separate ranged-attack target. RulesEngine.validate_shoot rejects the assignment, the AI shooting enumeration excludes it, and shooting the bodyguard folds the attached unit into a single allocation dialog (squad group first, CHARACTER group last).

RulesEngine.validate_shoot is the authoritative gate that rejects a SHOOT assignment whose target has attached_to set ('Cannot target X — attached character; shoot the bodyguard unit instead'), catching callers (the AI focus-fire plan) that bypass get_eligible_targets. Unattached characters and the bodyguard itself stay valid targets.

AIDecisionMaker._get_shootable_enemy_units excludes attached characters from _build_focus_fire_plan and the _decide_shooting fallback enumeration so the AI shoots the bodyguard squad instead of wasting an activation on a now-rejected shot at the leader. _get_enemy_units stays unfiltered for movement/charge/fight scoring.

When the squad leading a character is shot, the 11e AllocationGroupOverlay shows the combined Attached unit as ONE dialog titled with the bodyguard's display name: the squad's models as the first allocation group and the CHARACTER as the last group (reached only after the bodyguard, or via PRECISION).

CHARGE phase: Ctrl+Click adds a second target to a multi-charge even when the platform does not stamp ctrl_pressed onto the mouse button event. ChargeController owns target-list left-clicks via gui_input and detects Ctrl from BOTH the event and Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_CTRL), so a held Ctrl key toggles regardless of platform. Reproduced (pre-fix: held Ctrl key + unstamped click did not toggle, selection stayed at 1) and fixed via 'charge_ctrl_click_real_key' using the click_item_list ctrl_via_key mode.

In the Movement phase 'Units (Move / Disembark)' list, a bodyguard squad with an attached CHARACTER (e.g. a Blade Champion leading a Custodian Guard squad) names its leader exactly once in the row label. Regression for a bug where MovementController._refresh_unit_list() appended the attached-character names to the row twice (rendering 'Custodian Guard Alpha + Blade Champion + Blade Champion').

CHARGE phase Command/ability re-roll dialog: after a charge roll opens CommandRerollDialog, 'Keep Roll' is the highlighted primary button on the LEFT (ButtonRow child 0) and 'Re-roll (1 CP)' is the secondary button on the RIGHT (child 1) — the reverse of the old order — so a reflexive click on the prominent left-hand button no longer wastes a Command Point after a successful charge.

CommandRerollDialog._build_ui() builds the safe 'Keep Roll' decline button as the primary/highlighted control (font_size 16) and grabs keyboard focus on it, while the CP-spending 'Re-roll' button uses the secondary style (font_size 13). Shared by charge rolls, Command Re-roll and free ability re-rolls (e.g. Swift Onslaught), so the safe choice is the highlighted default everywhere the dialog appears.

The top-right phase-action button advertises the active input device's shortcut for ending a phase: the keyboard '[Enter] End Movement Phase' when KBM is active, and the pad Menu glyph '[☰] End Movement Phase' when a controller is active. The prefix comes from GlyphDB (single source of truth with the hint bar), re-stamps live on InputDeviceManager.device_changed without disturbing custom labels (roll-off Continue, Fight-phase step labels), and the pad confirm dialog reads the prefix-stripped action ('End Movement Phase?').

A controller player can discover how to end a phase without the touchscreen: the button they reach for names the Menu (☰) button bound to pad_phase_action, matching the '☰ End Phase' hint-bar chip, and pressing Menu (Start) pops the confirm dialog whose OK ends the phase.

The top-bar Stratagems button drops its keyboard '[S] ' hint while a controller is active ('Stratagems') and restores it for keyboard/mouse ('[S] Stratagems'), because a pad reaches the button via D-pad panel focus + A rather than an 'S' key. Re-stamped live on InputDeviceManager.device_changed and idempotent across repeated device switches.
